answers.answer:
Helios Sentinel employs a sophisticated multi-layered approach to real-time threat detection, combining signature-based matching, behavioral analysis, and ML-driven anomaly detection capabilities.

Our platform ingests data from multiple sources to provide comprehensive threat visibility:
• Endpoint telemetry including process events, file system changes, and network connections
• Cloud workload logs from AWS CloudTrail, Azure Activity Log, and GCP Audit Log
• Network flow data supporting NetFlow v9/IPFIX protocols
• Email gateway events for communication-based threats

Regarding detection latency, Helios Sentinel delivers industry-leading performance with:
• Average detection-to-alert latency of 2.3 seconds for signature-based matches
• 18 seconds average latency for behavioral detections requiring correlation analysis
• Processing capacity of up to 50,000 events per second per tenant through our SIEM correlation engine

Our detection accuracy is validated quarterly against the MITRE ATT&CK framework, maintaining a 99.7% true positive rate on our top 100 detection rules. This multi-faceted approach ensures rapid identification of both known threat patterns and novel attack behaviors while minimizing false positives.

Helios Security currently maintains the following compliance certifications:

• SOC 2 Type II - Most recent audit: December 2024 (conducted by Deloitte)
• ISO 27001:2022 - Certified: March 2024 (certified by BSI)
• FedRAMP Moderate - Authorized: June 2024 (sponsored by Department of Homeland Security)
• HIPAA - Last assessment: October 2024 (Business Associate Agreements available)
• PCI DSS v4.0 Level 1 Service Provider - Validated: September 2024 (validated by Coalfire)
• StateRAMP - Authorized: January 2025

All certifications are maintained on a continuous monitoring basis with quarterly internal audits to ensure ongoing compliance. We provide audit reports upon request under appropriate non-disclosure agreements. Our dedicated security team of 14 full-time engineers actively manages all compliance programs to maintain these certifications and ensure adherence to the highest security standards.

Helios Security offers the following per-seat pricing for our Endpoint Protection Platform (EPP+EDR bundle):

• 500 endpoints: $18/seat/month ($108,000/year)
• 1,000 endpoints: $15/seat/month ($180,000/year)
• 5,000 endpoints: $11/seat/month ($660,000/year)

Volume discounts are available and automatically applied at the following tiers:
• 1,000 endpoints: 17% volume discount compared to base pricing
• 5,000 endpoints: 39% volume discount compared to base pricing

Additional multi-year discounts are available:
• 2-year contracts: Additional 5% discount
• 3-year contracts: Additional 10% discount

The minimum contract term is 12 months. Optional add-ons include SIEM (+$6/seat/month) and MDR (+$12/seat/month). All pricing excludes professional services.

Helios Security currently serves 47 customers in the financial services vertical, representing a diverse portfolio across the sector. This includes 12 banks, 8 insurance carriers, 15 asset management firms, and 12 fintech companies.

We can provide the following reference accounts that are approved for external use:

1) **Meridian National Bank** - Deployed since 2022, protecting 3,200 endpoints with our comprehensive EPP+EDR+SIEM solution

2) **Crestview Capital Partners** - Deployed since 2023, securing 850 endpoints with our EPP+MDR services

3) **Apex Insurance Group** - Our longest-standing reference in this vertical, deployed since 2021, protecting 5,100 endpoints with our full security platform

Our financial services clients report high satisfaction levels, with an average Net Promoter Score of 72 in this vertical, reflecting our deep understanding of the unique security challenges and regulatory requirements facing financial institutions.

Helios Security provides comprehensive EU data residency compliance through dedicated infrastructure located in Frankfurt (AWS eu-central-1) and Dublin (AWS eu-west-1) regions. Customer data is guaranteed to never leave the selected EU region, ensuring full compliance with GDPR and other European data protection requirements.

Encryption at Rest: All customer data is encrypted using AES-256-GCM encryption with customer-managed keys. Customers can choose between AWS Key Management Service (KMS) or bring-your-own-key (BYOK) options for complete control over their encryption keys.

Encryption in Transit: All communications utilize TLS 1.3 encryption for API and agent communications. Our endpoint agents implement certificate pinning for additional security assurance during data transmission.

Additional EU Compliance Features: All EU contracts include a GDPR Data Processing Agreement (DPA). Data retention is configurable per tenant with default settings of 90 days for raw telemetry and 13 months for aggregated alerts. Our security posture is validated through annual third-party penetration testing conducted by NCC Group.

This architecture ensures that EU customers maintain complete data sovereignty while benefiting from enterprise-grade encryption both at rest and in transit.
sources: Helios Data Sovereignty & Privacy Whitepaper v3.1

issues.description: Q3 states SIEM add-on costs +$6/seat/month, but Q4 describes Meridian National Bank as having 'EPP+EDR+SIEM solution' without clarifying if this is an add-on or included service. This creates ambiguity about what's included in base pricing.
issues.suggested_fix: Clarify in Q4 that SIEM is an add-on service for the reference account, or specify if it's part of a custom bundle

issues.description: Q1 mentions 'SIEM correlation engine' as part of core platform capabilities, while Q3 lists SIEM as an optional add-on (+$6/seat/month). This suggests confusion about whether SIEM is included or additional.
issues.suggested_fix: Clarify that Q1 refers to basic correlation capabilities while Q3's SIEM add-on provides advanced SIEM functionality, or correct the inconsistency

issues.description: Q1 cites 'Acme Corp RFP Response -- March 2024' as a source, while Q4 references a different customer context. Using external RFP responses as sources may not be appropriate.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace with internal technical documentation sources consistent with other answers

issues.description: Q4 mentions EU-based customers implicitly (financial services), but doesn't cross-reference Q5's EU data residency capabilities which would be highly relevant for European financial institutions.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a brief mention in Q4 about EU data residency compliance for European financial services clients

review.overall_assessment: The answers are generally well-structured and professional with consistent tone throughout. However, there are some technical contradictions around SIEM capabilities and pricing that need clarification. The content appears comprehensive but requires alignment on whether SIEM is a core feature or add-on service.
